title: [phase:critique] [family:content-analyzer-diagnosis] [provider_hint:codex] Review /Users/masayuki/Dev/Content_Analyzer Stripe edge functions for the most likely cause of a Stripe connection error. Focus on SDK initialization, Deno compatibility, env key names, and network client setup. Return a conc...
summary_text: 1. High: the most likely cause of a real Stripe connection failure is the Stripe client being created with default transport inside Deno Edge, without explicitly pinning the fetch-based HTTP client. This pattern appears in [supabase/functions/create-checkout-session/index.ts:71](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/Content_Analyzer/supabase/functions/create-checkout-session/index.ts#L71), [supabase/functions/customer-portal/index.ts:75](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/Content_Analyzer/supabase/functions/customer-portal/index.ts#L75), and [supabase/functions/stripe-webhook/index.ts:35](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/Content_Analyzer/supabase/functions/stripe-webhook/index.ts#L35). `npm:stripe@17` itself is Deno-compatible, so the import target is probably fine; the gap is that these edge functions never set `httpClient: Stripe.createFetchHttpClient()`. In Supabase/Deno examples, Stripe is wired for Fetch/Web Crypto explicitly, so this is the strongest code-level explanation for a `StripeConnectionError`. 2. Medium: webhook verification is also missing the explicit Deno crypto provider. [supabase/functions/stripe-webhook/index.ts:56](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/Content_Analyzer/supabase/functions/stripe-webhook/index.ts#L56) calls `constructEventAsync(...)` correctly with the raw body, but does not pass `Stripe.createSubtleCryptoProvider()`. That is less likely than the transport issue to cause a generic connection error, but it is the main Deno-compatibility gap in the webhook path. 3. Medium: env naming is inconsistent and can easily lead to a broken deployment even if the code is otherwise correct. [.env.example:29](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/Content_Analyzer/.env.example#L29) still documents old keys like `STRIPE_PRICE_LITE` / `STRIPE_PRICE_PRO`, while the actual edge functions require `STRIPE_PRICE_LITE_MONTHLY`, `STRIPE_PRICE_LITE_YEARLY`, `STRIPE_PRICE_PRO_MONTHLY`, and `STRIPE_PRICE_PRO_YEARLY` in [supabase/functions/create-checkout-session/index.ts:13](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/Content_Analyzer/supabase/functions/create-checkout-session/index.ts#L13) and [supabase/functions/stripe-webhook/index.ts:14](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/Content_Analyzer/supabase/functions/stripe-webhook/index.ts#L14). [supabase/config.toml:35](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/Content_Analyzer/supabase/config.toml#L35) also only comments generic secrets and does not call out the Stripe ones, so `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` / `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` are easy to miss in Supabase project secrets. That would produce `"Stripe not configured"` or `"Invalid price ID"`, not a transport error, but it is the next most likely operational cause. Diagnosis: the top suspect is missing Deno-specific Stripe client setup, not the `npm:stripe@17` import itself. Reference: Stripe Deno support in `stripe-node` and Supabase’s Stripe webhook example, which uses explicit Fetch/Web Crypto wiring: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-node and https://supabase.com/docs/guides/functions/examples/stripe-webhooks
